definition: Compute embeddings for DNA sequences or allele-specific sequence contexts and use cosine similarity or distance as the overlap/novelty axis. For train-eval SPECTRA audits, compare each evaluation sequence embedding to training sequence embeddings; for counterfactual variant tasks, allele-embedding distance can be used as a variant perturbation magnitude axis.
example_usage: python embedding_cosine_similarity.py --train train_embeddings.csv --eval eval_embeddings.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --embedding-cols-prefix emb_

citations.title: DART-Eval: A Comprehensive DNA Language Model Evaluation Benchmark on Regulatory DNA

evidence.summary: The paper evaluates motif recognition using cosine embedding distance from neutral sequence backgrounds to positive and negative motif sequences.
The paper uses cosine distance between allele sequence embeddings as a zero-shot variant effect score.
The appendix specifies cosine distance as the embedding distance metric.

limitations: Embedding distance may underperform likelihood or fine-tuned models for regulatory DNA tasks.
Cosine geometry depends on model layer, pooling, context length, and tokenization.
Variant-effect allele distance is not automatically a train-eval novelty measure.
quality_gates: Record model, layer, pooling method, and context window.
Compare embedding axes against simpler motif or sequence-composition axes.
For variant tasks, separate train-eval novelty from allele perturbation magnitude.
